calendar.matters:
Petition To Enforce Money Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary David Goldberg; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Proposed Orders
Off Calendar Jun-28-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Spraggins for Department of Child Support.
Philip Goldberg (Trustee/Respondent) in Propria Persona.

The Court asks if there is any progress on sale of the property. Mr. Goldberg stated that there is not, that the property remains on the market, that they have had open houses, but have received no offers and that the price was reduced. Mr. Spraggins stated that there is $3,000.00 in the trust bank account, that there was a balance of $158,000.00 which existed after he filed his petition, that the amount was drawn down just before the hearing and that there was no explanation. Mr. Goldberg stated that there is a liquidity problem, that there was a loan from the trust to his Wife to provide for some of the financing for the down payment for the property, and that all trust funds have been applied and accounted for to provide for the dwelling. The Court raised the issue of whether the order can be conditioned on the sale of the house and in a form of a lien on the property. Mr. Goldberg stated that he does not know if the Court has the authority to do that, that the issue relates to liquidity, that if the Court does that the spend thrift trust will have will more obligation than it can fulfill, and that he has no objection to paying the support arears, but he can't do it today. Mr. Spraggins is to provide an alternate form of order that provides for payment from the proceeds from the sale in the form of a lien on the house. Mr. Goldberg may also provide an alternate form of order. The parties are to show their orders to each other. The orders are to be provided to the Court no later than July 3, 2019. Mr. Spraggins and Mr. Goldberg are also to e-mail their orders to the Court Clerk in the form of a word document. The Petition to Enforce Money Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition To Enforce Money Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary David Goldberg; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Proposed Orders
Off Calendar May-28-2019 Continued To Jun-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paul Spraggins for Department of Child Support.
Phillip Goldberg (Trustee) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Spraggins stated that he does not represent the Mother in this case, that he suggested to Mr. Goldberg to speak with the Mother to see if she would accept a different amount and that he has an obligation to collect what is on the books. Mr. Spraggins also stated that the Father is currently paying $325.00 a month in child support and $395.00 a month in arears, that the Father is terminal, that the obligator has the money to pay for child support, that he has $250,000.00 and that he owes $39,000.00. Mr. Goldberg stated that he has not reached out to the Mother, that there are no funds available, that there is $4,000.00 in cash and that temporary financing has been arranged by his Wife to purchase the North Carolina property. Mr. Goldberg also stated that the Goldberg property has not sold, that there is a liquidity problem, raised the issue of entering into some form of stipulation and stated that he does not feel speaking with the Mother would be fruitful. The Petition to Enforce Money Judgment Against Trust Beneficiary, etc. is continued to June 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to talk to each other and any others, and for the Parties to figure out how they will go forward.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
